description: Figure 4 shows a metallographic cross-section of a large oxide nodule formed on an Fe-Mn-Al alloy after isothermal oxidation at 800°C for 24 hr. The bright white region at the lower left is the alloy substrate, from which a bulky, irregular multilayered nodule protrudes upward into the dark mounting medium. The nodule displays distinct contrast bands indicating layered oxide growth. According to the caption, the alloy composition is Fe-7.5Mn-3Al, whereas the main text refers to Fe-7.5Mn-5Al for this micrograph. The cited text reports that the nodule consists of a white outer Fe2O3 layer and an inner (Fe,Mn)3O4 layer, with significant manganese and aluminum enrichment occurring in the inner layer and at the gas-oxide interface. Dark patches visible within the nodule suggest porosity or localized phase variations. This image illustrates the non-protective oxidation morphology characteristic of alloys with insufficient aluminum to form a continuous alumina scale, supporting the finding that ~7.5% Mn alloys develop large iron-rich nodules structurally akin to those on binary Fe-Al alloys.
